pattern: A running count of how many checkouts have happened is all that is needed to know whether the current one lands on every nth spot, at which point the discount is applied to that checkout's own subtotal only.
hints: Keep a lookup from product id to its price so each checkout's subtotal can be computed as a simple weighted sum.
Track a running count of checkouts across every call; a checkout gets the discount exactly when that count is a multiple of n.
The discount, when it applies, scales only the current checkout's subtotal by the remaining percentage after subtracting the discount.

edge_inputs.description: seven checkouts against a shared catalog, discount lands on the third
n equals one so every single checkout is discounted
a zero percent discount still triggers on the nth checkout but changes nothing
a full one hundred percent discount zeroes out the discounted checkout
a single checkout covering several distinct products at once
the discount lands only on the third of exactly three checkouts

stress.description: randomized catalog with many checkouts spanning several discount cycles
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num_products = rng.randint(1, 50)
    products = rng.sample(range(1, 201), num_products)
    prices = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in products]
    n = rng.randint(1, 20)
    discount = rng.randint(0, 100)
    ops = ['Cashier']
    args = [[n, discount, products, prices]]
    calls = min(max(1, size), 500)
    for _ in range(calls):
        k = rng.randint(1, min(10, len(products)))
        chosen = rng.sample(products, k)
        amounts = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in chosen]
        ops.append('getBill')
        args.append([chosen, amounts])
    return [ops, args]

complexity.time: O(k) per getBill call, k = number of distinct products in that checkout
